message:
fix(rpc): handle non-deterministic response ordering in abort_bash test

The rpc_abort_bash_kills_background_children test assumed abort_bash
and bash responses would arrive in a fixed order (abort first, then
bash). In practice the RPC server may deliver them in either order
depending on scheduling. This caused intermittent test failures.

Replaces the sequential send_recv + recv_line pattern with:
- Raw send for the abort command (no blocking recv)
- A loop that reads 2 responses and dispatches by (command, id)
- Pattern-matches abort_bash/id=2 and bash/id=1 regardless of order
- Panics on unexpected response types for clear failure diagnosis

The actual assertions (abort_resp ok, bash_resp ok with cancelled=true,
marker file absent after 4s) are unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
